(d) According to the question,
1 + 2 = 3
2 + 6 = 8
6 + 1 = 7
Clearly, it is not a standard dice.
∴ Digit at the opposite face of 2 = 3 or 4 or 5

(c) According to the question,
5 + 1 = 6
1 + 3 = 4
3 + 5 = 8
Clearly, it is a standard dice.
∴ Digit at the face opposite to 1 = 7 - 1 = 6

(b) According to the question,
5 + 1 = 6
1 + 3 = 4
3 + 5 = 8
Clearly, it is a standard dice.
∴ Digit at the face opposite to 3 = 7 - 3 = 4

(b) According to the question,
3 + 5 = 8
5 + 6 = 11
6 + 3 = 9
Clearly, it is a standard dice.
∴ Digit at the face opposite to 5 = 7 - 5 = 2

(d) According to the question,
4 + 3 = 7
4 + 2 = 6
3 + 2 = 5
Clearly, it is not a standard dice.
∴ Digit opposite 4 = 1 or 5 or 6
(d) The invisible number is 4 and it is opposite to number 2.

(d) Dice I → 2 3 4
Dice II → 2 1 4
So, opposite side of 1 is 3.

(c) In both the positions, 5 is at common face (front face).
So, digit 3 at the side face in position I will be opposite to the side face having digit 4 in position II.
(b) In the given two dices two numbers are common i.e., 3 and 5. So, remaining two numbers are opposite to each other Hence, number 2 is opposite to the number 4.
(b) From the two views of blocks it is clear that when 10 is at the bottom, number 12 will be at the top.

(b) From dice II and III, 3 and 5 are two common numbers. Hence, 6 and 4 are opposite to each other.
or
From second method (Dice I and II) (since, 3 is common)
[Figure showing dice arrangement with numbers 2, 4, 3, 6, 5, 1]
1 is opposite to 3.
4 is opposite to 6.
2 is opposite to 5.

(b) From positions II and III
Position II (clockwise) 5, 4, 2
Position III 5, 3, 1
Clearly, digit opposite to 3 = 4

(a) From positions I and III,
Common digits = 2 and 3
∴ Digit opposite to 6 = 1

(a) Expanded form of the dice is
[Figure showing expanded dice with numbers 3, 4, 6, 1, 5, 2]
Thus, X = 3

(c) From positions II and IV,
Common digit 6 is at the same (side) face in both the positions.
∴ Digit opposite to 3 = 4

(d) From the four views of the cube it is clear that
4 lies opposite 6.
2 lies opposite 5.
3 lies opposite 1.
Therefore, the number 3 will be in the place of question mark.
(d) It is quite clear from the positions I, II and IV that except 5, all numbers (6, 4, 3 and 1) are on adjacent faces of 2. Hence, 5 and 2 are opposite to each other.
(c) In both the positions, 5 dots are common at the same (Right) face. So, face having 4 dots will be opposite to face having 3 dots.
(b) In both the positions, 2 dots are common at the same (top) face. So, face having 1 dot will be opposite to the face having 6 dots.
(a) In both the positions, 4 dots are common at the same (top) face. So, face having 6 dots will be opposite to the face having 3 dots according to the clockwise direction.
